purpose: add_rule_from_json's weights dtype variants, riding np.array(weights, dtype=float64) conversion at the pin (rule_parser.py:208-212): plain ints, numeric strings ('0.5'), and booleans all convert and load; a rectangular NESTED list [[1,2]] is ACCEPTED too — np's len() is the top-level row count, so it passes a one-clause rule's length check (wd_nested in the closing fingerprint; weights contents stay unobserved at this seam, per the board). Raise arms, one fixture each: a non-convertible entry (['heavy']) raises builtins.Exception 'Item 0: Unexpected error - weights must be a numpy array or convertible to one, got list' — the parser's TypeError falls past the loader's ValueError catch into the Exception wrap; a length mismatch, a negative entry, and a bare NaN literal (json.load accepts it) each raise the single-wrapped loader ValueError with the parser's own text.
provenance: docs/surface.md fn:add_rule_from_json happy-weights/malformed-weights-* widening; pyreason.py:1036-1046 + rule_parser.py:203-230 at the pin; every arm screened live 2026-07-12 (4 fresh pin processes, digest-stable); fixtures harness/fixtures/rules-weights-*.json
